title: requests TLS bypass
subtitle: Session verify=False stickiness
query: Assess CVE-2024-35195 in the Python requests library and how urgently to patch.

data.query: Assess CVE-2024-35195 in the Python requests library and how urgently to patch.

data.summary: CVE-2024-35195 is a MEDIUM-severity (CVSS 5.6) flaw in Python `requests` < 2.32.0. A Session that first calls a host with verify=False silently disables TLS cert verification for all subsequent connections to that host in the pool, enabling potential MitM attacks even if later requests set verify=True.
data.recommended_action: SSVC: Track* — No public exploits, not in CISA KEV, EPSS very low (0.34%, 26th percentile). Patch within standard update timelines: upgrade to requests>=2.32.0 (`pip install --upgrade requests`). Interim: audit Session usage for mixed verify= settings; never set verify=False on an early request in a shared session. Ref: GHSA-9wx4-h78v-vm56.

cves.summary: In Python `requests` < 2.32.0, a Session that first calls a host with verify=False disables TLS certificate verification for all subsequent connections to that host in the connection pool, silently overriding any later verify=True — enabling man-in-the-middle attacks.

reasoning_chain: cve_lookup(CVE-2024-35195) -> CVSS 5.6 MEDIUM, CWE-670 (Always-Incorrect Control Flow), affects requests < 2.32.0
exploit_check(CVE-2024-35195) -> no public exploits on Exploit-DB or GitHub
kev_check(CVE-2024-35195) -> not in CISA KEV catalog
epss_score(CVE-2024-35195) -> probability=0.0034 (0.34%), percentile=0.26 — very low exploitation likelihood
patch_lookup(CVE-2024-35195) -> NVD CPE entries absent, but CVE text and references confirm fix in requests 2.32.0
attack_mapping([CWE-670]) -> no matching ATT&CK technique in curated table
Decision: no KEV, no public exploit, low EPSS -> SSVC Track* (standard timelines); recommend upgrade to requests>=2.32.0

ssvc.rationale: SSVC decision Track: CVE-2024-35195 no active-exploitation, public-exploit, or high-EPSS signal observed.
